summary: Russell carries momentum from his Austrian masterclass into Mercedes' Silverstone fortress, where he'll hunt pole position against Hamilton's Ferrari resurgence after the championship leader claimed sprint pole with a dominant practice display. McLaren and Red Bull circle for strategic opportunities in the sprint race format, with tire management and DRS deployment reshaping traditional Silverstone overtaking patterns under the new car regulations. A decisive result here could swing championship momentum decisively—Hamilton leads the standings, but Russell's maturity and Russell's team setup advantage at their home circuit offers Mercedes a genuine title-fight inflection point.
